title: Compress this veterinary-software market sizing to its investable thesis
guidance: Reward the version that connects the number to a thesis with a wedge. Penalise a version that restates the data.

parts.label: Thesis bullets
parts.note: 3: why now, why here, the wedge

runner_input.instruction: Compress the sizing into the market section of an investment memo. Connect the numbers to a thesis.
runner_input.constraints: Max 220 words. State SAM and growth. Explain why now and what the wedge is. Skip methodology.

source_blocks.text: UK independent veterinary practice management software. Sizing from RCVS practice counts: ~5,300 independent/small-group practices; ~70% (3,710) on dedicated practice-management software, ~30% (1,590) on generic tools or paper.

items: Current dedicated SAM: ~£190m (3,710 practices x ~£51k avg ACV)
Greenfield (switchers from generic/paper): ~£81m at same ACV
Total SAM: ~£271m, growing ~9% YoY on rising clinical-record and controlled-drug compliance
Incumbent weakness: two legacy vendors hold ~60% share, on-premise, last major release 4+ years ago; survey NPS ~12
Entrant tailwind: cloud + a new RCVS digital clinical-record guideline (2025) and pet-insurance API integrations lower switching cost

rubric_notes: The investable thesis: £271m SAM, low-NPS on-premise incumbents with no recent release, and a 2025 RCVS digital-record guideline + insurance APIs that lower switching cost.
The wedge is cloud + the RCVS guideline, not just market size.
Penalise leading with a big TAM disconnected from why displacement is achievable now.
